response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has extensive experience in software development, including C++ during his time at National Instruments, but his recent focus has been heavily on web and mobile technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, React, Ember.js, DLang) rather than C++ or industrial automation. His lack of recent C++ experience, no clear evidence of secure development lifecycle (SDL) or IEC 62443 standards exposure, and no documented experience with industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.) significantly reduce his fit for this role. However, his strong software engineering background, problem-solving skills, and agile collaboration could still make him a potential candidate with some adaptation.
strengths: Extensive software engineering background with 15+ years of diverse experience in multiple languages and paradigms
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills demonstrated through complex projects (e.g., GISCollective, robotic arms, low-latency applications)
Experience with agile methodologies, TDD, CI/CD, and DevOps practices (e.g., Docker, GitLab CI, trunk-based development)
concerns: Limited recent C++ experience (last notable use was 9 years ago at National Instruments; current stack is primarily TypeScript, JavaScript, and DLang)
No clear evidence of industrial automation, IoT, or industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443) experience in resume
Lacks exposure to key technical requirements like message brokers, multi-threading in C++, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.)
